title: einvoice conformance report (v1)
description: Machine-readable JSON Schema for the single-document EN 16931 / XRechnung conformance report produced by `python3 -m einvoice.report` / `einvoice.report.build_report`. This is the machine-checkable companion to the `REPORT_SCHEMA` constant in einvoice/report.py and to REPORT-SCHEMA.md. The report is a deterministic projection of einvoice.validate and adds no rule logic. Versioning policy: this $id and the `schema` const are STABLE within v1 - additive, backward-compatible fields may be added without changing them; any BREAKING change to the report shape bumps the id (and the const) to .../v2. Shape note (accurately reflected below): the plain-XML UBL path additionally carries the `syntax_bindings` array and its two count fields; the Factur-X/ZUGFeRD PDF (embedded-CII) path and the not-well-formed / unsupported-container error path do not, so those keys are OPTIONAL here while the nine core fields are required. The degenerate error report (carrying `error`/`message`, empty `violations`, no `syntax_bindings`) is accepted by allowing those optional keys.

schema.description: Stable schema id. Consumers should match on this exact string.

report_version.description: Report shape version; pinned to 1 for this schema.

source.description: The invoice path or label passed in.

valid.description: True iff there are zero FATAL violations (and, on the UBL path, zero fatal syntax-binding findings).

rule.description: The rule id, e.g. 'BR-DE-15' (Violation.rule_id); may also be an 'FX-CONTAINER-*' id on the PDF path.

field.description: The offending element / path (Violation.element); null when unknown.

title.description: Plain-language rule title relayed from the remediation catalog; null if the rule has no catalog entry.

fix_hint.description: One-line 'how to fix' guidance from the catalog; null if none.

terms.description: BT-/BG- business-term ids the rule touches; empty list if none.

location.description: The catalog's XML location/path hint; null if none.

source_line.description: OPTIONAL, additive on this same v1 schema id: the 1-based parser line of the offending element in the source document. Present ONLY for an attributable field-level violation (a rule that held the concrete Element, e.g. BR-CL-01/04/05 on a present-but-invalid code); ABSENT when the finding is not attributable to a source position (an absence/document-level rule, or any finding without a proven element). Distinct from the remediation-catalog XML-path hint 'location'. A consumer may ignore it safely.

syntax_bindings.description: The distinct syntax-binding category findings (UBL absence-restriction / cardinality caps). Present on the plain-XML UBL path; absent on the PDF/CII and error paths.

severity.description: 'fatal' blocks validity; every other flag maps to 'warning'.

flag.description: The raw official @flag value the severity was derived from.

message.description: The human message describing the syntax-binding restriction.

element.description: The XML path the finding targets.

syntax_binding_fatal_count.description: Number of syntax-binding findings with severity 'fatal'.

error.description: Present ONLY when the input cannot be reduced to a validatable invoice: a short code string ('not-well-formed' or 'unsupported-container'). 'valid' is then false and 'violations' is empty.

message.description: Present ONLY alongside 'error': the parser's / extractor's human message.
